name: Acacia mangium
description: Fast-growing tree species that dominates native forest areas and forms dense monocultures.
ecological_impact: Suppresses native vegetation, alters soil chemistry, reduces biodiversity.

name: Asian house rat
description: Invasive rat species that damages crops, stored food, and spreads multiple diseases.
ecological_impact: Destroys crops and stored food, spreads leptospirosis, plague, and other diseases.

name: Australian redclaw crayfish
description: Invasive freshwater crayfish that aggressively competes with native aquatic species.
ecological_impact: Outcompetes native aquatic species, disrupts freshwater ecosystems, destroys aquatic vegetation.

name: Banded bull frog
description: Invasive frog species that competes with native amphibians for food and habitat.
ecological_impact: Competes with native frogs, disrupts local food chains, spreads chytrid fungus.

name: Buyo-buyo
description: Armored suckermouth catfish invasive to Philippine waterways, also known as janitor fish.
ecological_impact: Destroys fish eggs, damages fishing nets, displaces native fish, disrupts river ecosystems.

name: Cane toad
description: Highly toxic invasive toad that is deadly to predators that attempt to eat it.
ecological_impact: Poisons native predators through toxic skin secretions, competes with native amphibians.

name: Chinese edible frog
description: Large invasive frog species that outcompetes native amphibian species for resources.
ecological_impact: Competes with native frogs for food and breeding habitat, may spread pathogens.

name: Crown of thorns
description: Coral-eating starfish that devastates reef ecosystems during population outbreaks.
ecological_impact: Destroys coral reefs by consuming coral polyps, reduces marine biodiversity significantly.

name: Fall armyworm
description: Highly destructive moth larvae that devastates crops across entire regions.
ecological_impact: Destroys corn, rice, sorghum and other staple crops, causes massive agricultural losses.

name: Giant african land snail
description: One of the world's most destructive land snails, consuming hundreds of plant species.
ecological_impact: Damages crops and vegetation, spreads rat lungworm disease, competes with native snails.

name: Golden apple snail
description: Highly invasive freshwater snail that devastates rice paddies across Southeast Asia.
ecological_impact: Destroys rice paddies, outcompetes native snail species, affects wetland ecosystems.

name: Greenhouse frog
description: Small invasive frog that disrupts native insect populations and food webs.
ecological_impact: Competes with native insectivores, alters arthropod communities, disrupts food webs.

name: House mouse
description: Invasive rodent that causes significant damage to crops, stored food, and native wildlife.
ecological_impact: Destroys stored food and crops, spreads diseases, preys on native bird eggs and insects.

name: Ipil-ipil
description: Fast-growing leguminous shrub that forms dense monocultures in disturbed areas.
ecological_impact: Suppresses native vegetation, reduces plant diversity, forms impenetrable thickets.

name: Nile tilapia
description: Aggressive invasive fish that dominates and alters freshwater ecosystems.
ecological_impact: Outcompetes native fish species, alters aquatic habitats, reduces native fish populations.

name: Walking catfish
description: Invasive catfish capable of breathing air and moving across land between water bodies.
ecological_impact: Outcompetes native fish, spreads rapidly across waterways, predates on native species.

name: Water hyacinth
description: Fast-growing aquatic plant that clogs waterways and devastates aquatic ecosystems.
ecological_impact: Reduces oxygen in water, blocks sunlight, kills fish, clogs irrigation systems.
